cvss_v3_1: 7.5 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H) [PYSEC-2026-178 detached-JWS DoS, worst-case of 6 PyJWT advisories]
description: PyJWT 2.11.0 carries 6 advisories (PYSEC-2026-120/175/176/177/178/179): crit-header bypass, PyJWK algorithm-confusion, JWKS-SSRF/file://, unbounded JWKS fetch, and a detached-JWS unauthenticated DoS. The app uses jwt.decode() with a STATIC HS256 secret and an explicitly pinned algorithms list, and does NOT use the PyJWK/PyJWKClient/jku flow, so the algorithm-confusion and JWKS-SSRF vectors are not reachable; the detached-JWS DoS and crit-header issues are the materially relevant ones.
remediation: Upgrade PyJWT to >=2.13.0. Declared explicitly in pyproject.toml (PyJWT>=2.13.0,<3.0) and installed (verified 2.13.0).

file_ref: pyproject.toml (transitive: python-multipart 0.0.22); used by backend/app/api/documents.py:107 ingestDocument upload
description: python-multipart <0.0.27 parses multipart/form-data part headers and preamble/epilogue without count/size limits (CVE-2026-40347, CVE-2026-42561), enabling CPU-exhaustion DoS on the file-upload endpoint. This path is directly reachable by an authenticated caller uploading a crafted PDF multipart body.
remediation: Upgrade python-multipart to >=0.0.27. Declared explicitly in pyproject.toml (python-multipart>=0.0.27) and installed (verified 0.0.32).

description: Starlette <1.0.1 reconstructs request.url from an unvalidated Host header; middleware/endpoints that gate on request.url.path can be bypassed. This app authorizes on tenant_id from the credential and the raw scope path (not request.url.path), so the bypass vector is not used by current code, but the vulnerable component is in the runtime closure.
remediation: Floor starlette>=1.0.1 in pyproject.toml. FastAPI permits starlette>=0.46.0, so the bump is compatible; installed and verified 1.2.1. Full test suite re-run green (265 passed, 1 skipped).

description: python-dotenv <1.2.2 follows symlinks in set_key()/unset_key() on a cross-device rename fallback, allowing local arbitrary-file overwrite. This application only READS .env via pydantic-settings and never calls set_key/unset_key, so the vector is not reachable in current code; the vulnerable component is nonetheless in the runtime closure.
remediation: Floor python-dotenv>=1.2.2 in pyproject.toml. Config pin applied; a clean install / CI run installs the patched version. In-place upgrade of the shared host interpreter was blocked by an external process holding dotenv/__pycache__ (Windows file lock) at audit time, so the running env still reports 1.1.1. Not exploitable by this app (no write path). Verification deferred to the next clean install (CI).

cvss_v3_1: 5.3 (AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N) [GHSA-67mh-4wv8-2f99 esbuild dev-server]; 1 npm-'critical' is a metadata rollup of the same vite/esbuild chain
description: npm audit reports 5 vulnerabilities (4 moderate, 1 'critical') entirely within DEV dependencies (esbuild/vite/vite-node/@vitest/mocker/vitest). The esbuild advisory affects only the local Vite dev server. Production deps (react, react-dom) audit clean (npm audit --omit=dev = 0). The production build is a static asset bundle that ships no dev server and no esbuild runtime.
remediation: The esbuild fix (>=0.25.0) is only available via vite v7+/vitest v4+, a breaking multi-major upgrade of the test toolchain that risks regressing the green suite, for a dev-only issue not present in the production bundle. Accepted as a documented exception. CI gate added: 'npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=low' blocks any PRODUCTION-dep vulnerability. Recommend a scheduled vite/vitest v7/v4 migration outside the security gate.

description: Bandit -ll flagged 0 issues. A full low-severity pass enumerated 227 LOW findings: 226 are B101 assert_used, all located inside test modules (router/tests, db/tests, ingestion/tests). assert in tests is intended and excluded by the CI bandit config (exclude_dirs=['tests']) and by ruff per-file-ignore S101.
remediation: No action required. Production code (backend, ingestion, router, db non-test) has zero Bandit findings at any severity.

description: Bandit B105 (1) flagged a regex literal '^[0-9]{1,3}%$' as a hardcoded password (already annotated # noqa: S105). detect-secrets (5) flagged: a regex 'Secret Keyword' in schemas.py, two test-only literals in conftest.py (JWT_SECRET/API_KEY_SALT, annotated # noqa: S105), a 'USER:PASSWORD' DSN placeholder in migrations/README.md, and a placeholder in an accessibility report. None are real secrets; all are placeholders, test fixtures, or regex patterns.
remediation: No action required. No real credential is committed. .env is gitignored; provider keys and JWT_SECRET/API_KEY_SALT are env-injected. CI detect-secrets gate remains blocking for any future real secret.

description: Auth review confirmed positive controls: JWT decoded with algorithms=[settings.jwt_algorithm] (alg=none and algorithm-confusion rejected), options require ['exp','sub'], audience and issuer validated, missing tenant claim -> 401. API keys compared as salted HMAC-SHA-256 digests; plaintext never stored; rotation deactivates the old digest. No defect.
remediation: None. Recommend (non-blocking) documenting that the live API-key store must be backed by Postgres in production (the in-memory ApiKeyStore is the interface; stores.py provides the persistent backing).

description: Every document-scoped path resolves the record via store.get_document(principal.tenant_id, doc_id) and returns 404 when the id does not belong to the tenant (cross-tenant access is indistinguishable from not-found, preventing enumeration). Ingestion stamps tenant_id on document, sections, and every chunk payload; empty tenant_id raises ValueError. No defect.

Router prompt fences untrusted TOC+query, declares them non-instructions, forbids tool use / instruction-following / prompt disclosure, and constrains output to a TOC-derived allow-list. parse_router_llm_json strictly validates the reply (size-bounded, JSON object, extra='forbid', confidence range, ranked-item cap, section-id pattern) and falls back deterministically on any deviation. No defect.

Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build, creates a system 'app' user and runs USER app (non-root), bakes no secrets, and healthchecks /health. docker-compose injects all secrets from the developer .env at runtime (never baked). .env.example holds only placeholders; .env and *.pem/*.key are gitignored. No defect.
remediation: None. Recommend (non-blocking, F.4): require Qdrant API-key/network ACL and Postgres TLS in the production deployment manifest; enforce TLS termination at the edge (HSTS).

description: DPDP controls present: §8 erasure (DELETE /v1/documents/{id} -> tombstone + outbox), §8 access (GET /v1/documents/{id}/data -> document + sections + x-pii field-name inventory, never PII values), no-retention purge mode (skips persistence, retains no bytes), and residency_region tagging. PII fields annotated x-pii in openapi.yaml.
remediation: Recommendations (non-blocking, tracked for production hardening): (1) centralized append-only audit log on erasure/export/ingest writes (A09); (2) CERT-In 6-hour incident-reporting hook wired to an alerting channel; (3) confirm erasure-outbox reconciliation sweep is scheduled in the deploy manifest.
